methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $70M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Dusty Robotics is a Series B-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $70M+ to date. The peer universe consists of nine private companies at the same stage and sector, plus one public comparable, providing meaningful triangulation across a fragmented frontier-tech landscape.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement to the $70M total raised proxy, yielding $133M. Peer-median analysis examines six same-sector, same-stage comparables spanning $300M to $10.0B, with a median valuation of $1.1B. The wide range reflects substantial heterogeneity within Series B robotics: 1X Technologies trades at $10.0B while Fairmat sits at $350M, indicating either material differences in technology risk, go-to-market maturity, or capital efficiency.

Triangulating these two methods produces a range of $113M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $604M. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively to actual capital deployed; the peer-median method reflects market pricing but exhibits high dispersion. Key uncertainties include Dusty's relative position within the robotics value chain, product commercialization timeline, and whether peer valuations reflect sustainable unit economics or venture-stage pricing momentum. The $604M median implies meaningful but not outsized premium to capital raised, appropriate for a Series B in a capital-intensive sector with protracted development cycles.
